channel.title: Code Defence Intelligence Brief — Wed, 11 Mar 2026
channel.description: Daily cybersecurity intelligence and LinkedIn-ready executive posts for Wed, 11 Mar 2026.

item.title: Your public cloud portals are being systematically drained of corporate data. 🐚
item.cd:imagebrief: Dramatic dark-blue cinematic editorial. A digital representation of a cloud portal fracturing into glowing red data streams. Deep navy and electric cyan palette. Bold white sans-serif overlay text: 'CLOUD DATA THEFT ACTIVE.' Code Defence logo bottom-right corner. 1200×628px.
item.description:
Your public cloud portals are being systematically drained of corporate data. 🐚
Data Theft Alert · ShinyHunters exploiting Salesforce Experience Cloud misconfigurations.
In the last 24 hours, evidence has surfaced of a massive data theft campaign targeting @[Salesforce](urn:li:organization:1509) Experience Cloud sites. The ShinyHunters group is utilizing a modified version of the AuraInspector tool to identify misconfigured guest user permissions, allowing them to exfiltrate records from over 300 companies.
While @[Salesforce](urn:li:organization:1509) maintains the platform itself is secure, the issue stems from customer-configured guest access settings that allow unauthenticated users to query sensitive data. Attackers have bypassed previous record-limit restrictions and are actively harvesting data using the user agent Anthropic/RapeForceV2.01.39.
The uncomfortable truth: Your cloud collaboration platforms are the new primary frontier for mass data exfiltration if your default permissions are not set to Private.
→ Review and restrict Salesforce Experience Cloud guest user permissions immediately. → Disable 'Portal User Visibility' and 'Site User Visibility' in your global sharing settings. → Audit your web logs for unusual GraphQL API queries originating from unknown user agents.
Have you audited your public cloud portal configurations for overly permissive guest access this month? 👇
#Cybersecurity #CloudSecurity #Salesforce #DataBreach #CISO #CodeDefence

item.title: March Patch Tuesday fixes two zero-days and 79 security flaws. 🛡️
item.cd:imagebrief: Cinematic editorial style. A digital shield icon with 79 glowing patch points. Deep navy and electric cyan palette. Bold white sans-serif overlay text: 'PATCH TUESDAY ALERT.' Code Defence logo bottom-right corner. 1200×628px.
item.description:
March Patch Tuesday fixes two zero-days and 79 security flaws. 🛡️
March 2026 Patch Tuesday · 79 Vulnerabilities · 2 Publicly Disclosed Zero-Days.
@[Microsoft](urn:li:organization:1035) has released its March 2026 security update, addressing critical vulnerabilities across the Windows ecosystem. The update includes fixes for two zero-days: an elevation of privilege flaw in SQL Server (CVE-2026-21262) and a denial of service vulnerability in .NET (CVE-2026-26127).
While neither zero-day has confirmed active exploitation in the wild as of this morning, the public disclosure significantly increases the risk of rapid weaponization. The update also includes 18 remote code execution (RCE) fixes, with several labeled as critical impact for Office and Windows Graphics components.
The uncomfortable truth: Your enterprise endpoints are currently vulnerable to multiple RCE and privilege escalation paths that are now public knowledge.
→ Apply the March 2026 Microsoft security updates to all Windows endpoints and servers immediately. → Prioritize the patching of SQL Server and .NET environments due to the zero-day disclosures. → Ensure all Microsoft Office installations are updated to address critical RCE flaws in Excel and Word.
Is your automated patching cycle fast enough to deploy these critical fixes within the next 24 hours? 👇
#Cybersecurity #PatchTuesday #Microsoft #VulnerabilityManagement #SOC #CodeDefence

item.title: CISA flags SolarWinds and Ivanti management tools as actively exploited. 🔓
item.cd:imagebrief: Graphic design poster style. A digital toolkit icon fracturing into digital dust. Charcoal grey and warning yellow palette. Bold white sans-serif overlay text: 'MANAGEMENT TOOLS EXPLOITED.' Code Defence logo bottom-right corner. 1200×628px.
item.description:
CISA flags SolarWinds and Ivanti management tools as actively exploited. 🔓
CVE-2025-26399 · Severity 9.8 · Deserialization RCE in SolarWinds Web Help Desk.
The @[CISA](urn:li:organization:13010360) has added critical vulnerabilities in @[SolarWinds](urn:li:organization:166292) Web Help Desk and @[Ivanti](urn:li:organization:36124) Endpoint Manager (CVE-2026-1603) to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Reports indicate that ransomware groups are actively exploiting the SolarWinds flaw to achieve initial access and execute commands on host machines.
Management platforms are high-value targets because they often possess deep administrative privileges across the entire enterprise. A compromise here allows an attacker to move laterally with ease and disable security controls across the network.
The uncomfortable truth: The tools you use to manage your network are currently the most effective way for an attacker to dismantle it.
→ Patch SolarWinds Web Help Desk and Ivanti Endpoint Manager to the latest resolved versions immediately. → Strictly isolate all management interfaces from the public internet using secure VPNs or zero-trust gateways. → Monitor for unauthorized credential access or anomalous administrative command execution originating from these servers.
Are you managing your support and management tools with the same rigor as your production servers? 👇
#Cybersecurity #VulnerabilityManagement #SolarWinds #Ivanti #SOC #CodeDefence

item.title: Critical Nginx UI vulnerability allows unauthenticated backup theft. 🐚
item.cd:imagebrief: Data-viz style. A digital folder icon with glowing orange data leaks appearing over a web server interface. Matte black and deep gold palette. Bold white sans-serif overlay text: 'BACKUPS EXPOSED.' Code Defence logo bottom-right corner. 1200×628px.
item.description:
Critical Nginx UI vulnerability allows unauthenticated backup theft. 🐚
CVE-2026-27944 · Severity 9.8 · Unauthenticated Backup Download in Nginx UI.
A critical vulnerability in Nginx UI has been confirmed as under active exploitation. The flaw allows unauthenticated attackers to download and decrypt full system backups, exposing SSL private keys, administrative credentials, and session tokens.
Automated scanners are currently targeting Nginx UI management portals to harvest these backups. Because this allows for full server impersonation and administrative takeover, it represents a maximum-severity risk for any organization running an exposed Nginx UI instance.
The uncomfortable truth: Your backup strategy is an asset for an attacker if your management tool leaves the encryption keys exposed to the public internet.
→ Update Nginx UI to the latest security version and ensure the backup API endpoint is restricted. → Restrict access to all management interfaces through private networks or secure zero-trust tunnels. → Rotate any SSL private keys and administrative credentials if your portal was internet-exposed.
Have you verified that your web server management tools are hidden from public automated scanners? 👇
#Cybersecurity #WebSecurity #Nginx #DataPrivacy #SecurityLeadership #CodeDefence
